  F4  → F5, F7, F8 (tools, sessions, multilingual prompts)
  F3  → F11 (repo_registry, indexed_documents tables)
  F2  → F3, F4 (indexed content, agent runtime)
  F10 → F4, F9 (runtime, auth middleware)
  F1  → F10 (API endpoints)
  F6  → F10, F9 (admin API, admin auth)
```

## Feature List

### F11: Database Schema

- **Priority:** 1
- **Phase:** 1 — Foundation
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** None
- **Blocks:** F9, F12, F5, F3
- **User Stories:** US-01, US-02
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 8 (Data Model MVP), Section 5 (Required Technology Stack — Prisma/Postgres)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Prisma schema with all MVP tables (users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs)
  - Database migration files
  - Seed script for initial data (EdLight repos in repo_registry, initiatives)
  - Prisma client generation and type exports
  - Environment config with Zod validation for database connection

### F8: Multilingual Foundation

- **Priority:** 2
- **Phase:** 1 — Foundation
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** None
- **Blocks:** F4
- **User Stories:** US-03, US-04
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Multilingual Foundation), Section 5 (Required Technology Stack)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Language enum (en, fr, ht) and type definitions
  - Language detection/selection utilities
  - System prompt templates with language-specific instructions
  - Response language formatting helpers
  - Zod schemas for language input validation

### F12: Security and Audit Layer

- **Priority:** 3
- **Phase:** 1 — Foundation
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11
- **Blocks:** F5
- **User Stories:** US-05, US-06
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 12 (Security Constraints)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - SandraError base class and subclasses (ValidationError, AuthError, ToolError, NotFoundError)
  - Permission scope definitions (read:public_content, admin:indexing)
  - Scope enforcement utility for tool execution
  - Audit logging service (tool calls, admin actions)
  - Structured JSON error response formatter

### F9: Authentication Integration

- **Priority:** 4
- **Phase:** 2 — Auth & Sessions
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11
- **Blocks:** F7, F10, F6
- **User Stories:** US-07, US-08
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 5 (Firebase Auth), Section 12 (Security Constraints)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Firebase Admin SDK initialization
  - Token verification middleware for API routes
  - User resolution flow (Firebase UID → Postgres user record)
  - Admin role checking utility
  - Environment config for Firebase credentials (Zod validated)

### F7: Conversation Session Management

- **Priority:** 5
- **Phase:** 2 — Auth & Sessions
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F9
- **Blocks:** F4
- **User Stories:** US-09, US-10
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 7 (User Story 2 — session continuity), Section 8 (Firestore Collections)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Firestore client initialization and config
  - Session creation and retrieval service
  - Message persistence in chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages subcollection
  - Session context loader for agent runtime (returns recent message history)
  - Session ID generation utility

### F5: Tool-Based Architecture

- **Priority:** 6
- **Phase:** 3 — Agent Core
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11, F12
- **Blocks:** F4
- **User Stories:** US-11, US-12
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 9 (Tool Contracts MVP), Section 3 (Tool-Based Architecture)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Tool interface (name, description, inputSchema, requiredScopes, handler)
  - Tool registry with self-registration pattern
  - Tool executor with permission scope enforcement and logging
  - MVP tool implementations: searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus
  - Zod schemas for tool input validation

### F4: Sandra Agent Runtime

- **Priority:** 7
- **Phase:** 3 — Agent Core
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F5, F7, F8
- **Blocks:** F2, F10
- **User Stories:** US-13, US-14
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Sandra Agent Runtime), Section 2 (Implementation Goal)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - ReAct-style agent loop (receive → context → LLM → tool call loop → respond)
  - LLM provider abstraction interface (AIProvider)
  - OpenAI provider implementation with tool-calling support
  - System prompt builder (incorporates language, session context, available tools)
  - Channel normalization: InboundMessage/OutboundMessage types with web adapter
  - Agent action logging

### F3: Repository Indexing Pipeline

- **Priority:** 8
- **Phase:** 4 — Knowledge Pipeline
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11
- **Blocks:** F2
- **User Stories:** US-15, US-16
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 11 (Repository Indexing Requirements), Section 3 (Repository Indexing)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - GitHub API client for fetching README and documentation files
  - Document text extractor (markdown)
  - Recursive character text splitter with markdown-aware boundaries
  - Embedding generation service (via AIProvider abstraction)
  - Vector store abstraction interface with in-memory implementation
  - Index job tracking (create, update status) in Postgres
  - Metadata storage in indexed_sources and indexed_documents tables

### F2: Public Knowledge Question Answering

- **Priority:** 9
- **Phase:** 4 — Knowledge Pipeline
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F3, F4
- **Blocks:** F10
- **User Stories:** US-17, US-18
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Public Knowledge Question Answering), Section 7 (User Story 1)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Retrieval service: query embedding → vector similarity search → ranked results
  - Context injection into agent prompt (retrieved chunks with source attribution)
  - searchKnowledgeBase tool wired to retrieval service
  - Response generation with RAG context
  - End-to-end test: question about EdLight → retrieval → contextual answer

### F10: API Gateway

- **Priority:** 10
- **Phase:** 5 — Integration & UI
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F4, F9
- **Blocks:** F1, F6
- **User Stories:** US-19, US-20
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 10 (API Contracts)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - POST /api/sandra/chat — main chat endpoint (streaming via SSE)
  - GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId — retrieve session messages
  - GET /api/sandra/repos — list registered repositories
  - POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index — trigger indexing (admin-only)
  - GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId — indexing job status (admin-only)
  - GET /api/sandra/health — health check
  - Standard JSON envelope (success/error) with request IDs
  - Auth middleware integration on protected routes

### F1: Web Chat Interface

- **Priority:** 11
- **Phase:** 5 — Integration & UI
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F10
- **Blocks:** None
- **User Stories:** US-21, US-22, US-23
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Web Chat Interface), Section 7 (User Story 1, User Story 2)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Chat page with message input and assistant response rendering
  - Streaming response display (token-by-token rendering)
  - Loading states and error handling UI
  - Session persistence (session ID in URL or local storage)
  - Empty/welcome state with suggested questions
  - Language selector (English, French, Haitian Creole)
  - Responsive layout with EdLight branding-ready structure

### F6: Admin Indexing Controls

- **Priority:** 12
- **Phase:** 5 — Integration & UI
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F10, F9

- **User Stories:** US-24, US-25

- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Admin Indexing Controls), Section 7 (User Story 3)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Admin page listing registered repositories with indexing status
  - Index trigger button per repository
  - Job status display (polling or realtime updates)
  - Admin role gate (redirect unauthenticated/unauthorized users)

# Sandra V1 User Stories

## Summary

25 user stories across 6 categories.

## Traceability Matrix

| US ID | Title | Feature | Task(s) | Status |
|-------|-------|---------|---------|--------|
| US-01 | Database schema setup | F11 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-02 | Seed initial data | F11 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-03 | Receive responses in preferred language | F8 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-04 | Language types and validation | F8 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-05 | Structured error responses | F12 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-06 | Tool execution audit trail | F12 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-07 | Authenticate to access Sandra | F9 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-08 | Admin role verification | F9 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-09 | Conversation persists during session | F7 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-10 | Resume a conversation | F7 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-11 | Tool registry for agent functionality | F5 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-12 | Scoped tool execution | F5 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-13 | Agent processes user message | F4 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-14 | Agent uses tools when needed | F4 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-15 | Index repository documentation | F3 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-16 | Track indexing job progress | F3 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-17 | Ask about EdLight initiatives | F2 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-18 | Answer uses indexed knowledge | F2 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-19 | Chat API endpoint | F10 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-20 | Admin API endpoints | F10 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-21 | Chat with Sandra in browser | F1 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-22 | See streaming responses | F1 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-23 | Select language in chat UI | F1 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-24 | Trigger repository indexing | F6 | TBD | [ ] |
| US-25 | View indexing status | F6 | TBD | [ ] |

---

## Stories by Category

### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)

#### US-01: Database Schema Setup

> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-02: Seed Initial Data

> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language

> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-04: Language Types and Validation

> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-05: Structured Error Responses

> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail

> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)

#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra

> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-08: Admin Role Verification

> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session

> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-10: Resume a Conversation

> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)

#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality

> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution

> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed

> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress

> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives

> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge

> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints

> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)

#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser

> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-22: See Streaming Responses

> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI

> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
- [ ] The default language is English

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing

> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-25: View Indexing Status

> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

## Stories by Category

### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)

#### US-01: Database Schema Setup

> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-02: Seed Initial Data

> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language

> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-04: Language Types and Validation

> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-05: Structured Error Responses

> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail

> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)

#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra

> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-08: Admin Role Verification

> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session

> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-10: Resume a Conversation

> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)

#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality

> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution

> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed

> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress

> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives

> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge

> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints

> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)

#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser

> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-22: See Streaming Responses

> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI

> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
- [ ] The default language is English

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing

> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-25: View Indexing Status

> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

**Features** (`docs/implementation/sandra-v1/features.md`): 12 features across 5 phases:
- **Phase 1 — Foundation:** F11 (Database Schema), F8 (Multilingual), F12 (Security/Audit)

**User Stories** (`docs/implementation/sandra-v1/user_stories.md`): 25 stories across 6 categories, each with 3-6 testable acceptance criteria. All stories trace back to features; all features have at least one story. Both personas (Visitor, Administrator) are covered, plus Developer stories for infrastructure features.

# Sandra V1 Feature Registry

## Overview

12 features organized in 5 phases. Features must be built in dependency order, following the 13-milestone sequence defined in PRD section 13.

## Dependency Diagram

```
Phase 1: Foundation           Phase 2: Auth & Sessions
┌──────┐ ┌──────┐ ┌──────┐   ┌──────┐   ┌──────┐
│  F11 │ │  F8  │ │  F12 │   │  F9  │   │  F7  │
│Schema│ │ i18n │ │Secur.│   │ Auth │   │Sessn.│
└──┬───┘ └──┬───┘ └──┬───┘   └──┬───┘   └──┬───┘
   │        │        │          │           │
   │        │        │     ┌────┘           │
   │        │        │     │                │
   ▼        ▼        ▼     ▼                ▼
Phase 3: Agent Core           Phase 4: Knowledge
┌──────┐   ┌──────┐          ┌──────┐   ┌──────┐
│  F5  │──▶│  F4  │          │  F3  │──▶│  F2  │
│Tools │   │Runtm.│          │Index │   │  QA  │
└──┬───┘   └──┬───┘          └──────┘   └──┬───┘
   │          │                             │
   └──────────┼─────────────────────────────┘
              ▼
Phase 5: Integration & UI
┌──────┐   ┌──────┐   ┌──────┐
│  F10 │──▶│  F1  │   │  F6  │
│  API │   │ Chat │   │Admin │
└──────┘   └──────┘   └──────┘

Dependency Arrows:
  F9  → F11 (users table)
  F12 → F11 (audit_logs, tool_executions tables)
  F7  → F9  (user identity for sessions)
  F5  → F11, F12 (tool_executions table, permission scopes)
  F4  → F5, F7, F8 (tools, sessions, multilingual prompts)
  F3  → F11 (repo_registry, indexed_documents tables)
  F2  → F3, F4 (indexed content, agent runtime)
  F10 → F4, F9 (runtime, auth middleware)
  F1  → F10 (API endpoints)
  F6  → F10, F9 (admin API, admin auth)
```

## Feature List

### F11: Database Schema

- **Priority:** 1
- **Phase:** 1 — Foundation
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** None
- **Blocks:** F9, F12, F5, F3
- **User Stories:** US-01, US-02
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 8 (Data Model MVP), Section 5 (Required Technology Stack — Prisma/Postgres)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Prisma schema with all MVP tables (users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs)
  - Database migration files
  - Seed script for initial data (EdLight repos in repo_registry, initiatives)
  - Prisma client generation and type exports
  - Environment config with Zod validation for database connection

### F8: Multilingual Foundation

- **Priority:** 2
- **Phase:** 1 — Foundation
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** None
- **Blocks:** F4
- **User Stories:** US-03, US-04
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Multilingual Foundation), Section 5 (Required Technology Stack)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Language enum (en, fr, ht) and type definitions
  - Language detection/selection utilities
  - System prompt templates with language-specific instructions
  - Response language formatting helpers
  - Zod schemas for language input validation

### F12: Security and Audit Layer

- **Priority:** 3
- **Phase:** 1 — Foundation
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11
- **Blocks:** F5
- **User Stories:** US-05, US-06
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 12 (Security Constraints)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - SandraError base class and subclasses (ValidationError, AuthError, ToolError, NotFoundError)
  - Permission scope definitions (read:public_content, admin:indexing)
  - Scope enforcement utility for tool execution
  - Audit logging service (tool calls, admin actions)
  - Structured JSON error response formatter

### F9: Authentication Integration

- **Priority:** 4
- **Phase:** 2 — Auth & Sessions
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11
- **Blocks:** F7, F10, F6
- **User Stories:** US-07, US-08
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 5 (Firebase Auth), Section 12 (Security Constraints)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Firebase Admin SDK initialization
  - Token verification middleware for API routes
  - User resolution flow (Firebase UID → Postgres user record)
  - Admin role checking utility
  - Environment config for Firebase credentials (Zod validated)

### F7: Conversation Session Management

- **Priority:** 5
- **Phase:** 2 — Auth & Sessions
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F9
- **Blocks:** F4
- **User Stories:** US-09, US-10
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 7 (User Story 2 — session continuity), Section 8 (Firestore Collections)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Firestore client initialization and config
  - Session creation and retrieval service
  - Message persistence in chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages subcollection
  - Session context loader for agent runtime (returns recent message history)
  - Session ID generation utility

### F5: Tool-Based Architecture

- **Priority:** 6
- **Phase:** 3 — Agent Core
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11, F12
- **Blocks:** F4
- **User Stories:** US-11, US-12
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 9 (Tool Contracts MVP), Section 3 (Tool-Based Architecture)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Tool interface (name, description, inputSchema, requiredScopes, handler)
  - Tool registry with self-registration pattern
  - Tool executor with permission scope enforcement and logging
  - MVP tool implementations: searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus
  - Zod schemas for tool input validation

### F4: Sandra Agent Runtime

- **Priority:** 7
- **Phase:** 3 — Agent Core
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F5, F7, F8
- **Blocks:** F2, F10
- **User Stories:** US-13, US-14
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Sandra Agent Runtime), Section 2 (Implementation Goal)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - ReAct-style agent loop (receive → context → LLM → tool call loop → respond)
  - LLM provider abstraction interface (AIProvider)
  - OpenAI provider implementation with tool-calling support
  - System prompt builder (incorporates language, session context, available tools)
  - Channel normalization: InboundMessage/OutboundMessage types with web adapter
  - Agent action logging

### F3: Repository Indexing Pipeline

- **Priority:** 8
- **Phase:** 4 — Knowledge Pipeline
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F11
- **Blocks:** F2
- **User Stories:** US-15, US-16
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 11 (Repository Indexing Requirements), Section 3 (Repository Indexing)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - GitHub API client for fetching README and documentation files
  - Document text extractor (markdown)
  - Recursive character text splitter with markdown-aware boundaries
  - Embedding generation service (via AIProvider abstraction)
  - Vector store abstraction interface with in-memory implementation
  - Index job tracking (create, update status) in Postgres
  - Metadata storage in indexed_sources and indexed_documents tables

### F2: Public Knowledge Question Answering

- **Priority:** 9
- **Phase:** 4 — Knowledge Pipeline
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F3, F4
- **Blocks:** F10
- **User Stories:** US-17, US-18
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Public Knowledge Question Answering), Section 7 (User Story 1)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Retrieval service: query embedding → vector similarity search → ranked results
  - Context injection into agent prompt (retrieved chunks with source attribution)
  - searchKnowledgeBase tool wired to retrieval service
  - Response generation with RAG context
  - End-to-end test: question about EdLight → retrieval → contextual answer

### F10: API Gateway

- **Priority:** 10
- **Phase:** 5 — Integration & UI
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F4, F9
- **Blocks:** F1, F6
- **User Stories:** US-19, US-20
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 10 (API Contracts)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - POST /api/sandra/chat — main chat endpoint (streaming via SSE)
  - GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId — retrieve session messages
  - GET /api/sandra/repos — list registered repositories
  - POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index — trigger indexing (admin-only)
  - GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId — indexing job status (admin-only)
  - GET /api/sandra/health — health check
  - Standard JSON envelope (success/error) with request IDs
  - Auth middleware integration on protected routes

### F1: Web Chat Interface

- **Priority:** 11
- **Phase:** 5 — Integration & UI
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F10
- **Blocks:** None
- **User Stories:** US-21, US-22, US-23
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Web Chat Interface), Section 7 (User Story 1, User Story 2)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Chat page with message input and assistant response rendering
  - Streaming response display (token-by-token rendering)
  - Loading states and error handling UI
  - Session persistence (session ID in URL or local storage)
  - Empty/welcome state with suggested questions
  - Language selector (English, French, Haitian Creole)
  - Responsive layout with EdLight branding-ready structure

### F6: Admin Indexing Controls

- **Priority:** 12
- **Phase:** 5 — Integration & UI
- **Status:** [ ] Not started
- **Depends on:** F10, F9
- **Blocks:** None
- **User Stories:** US-24, US-25
- **Tasks:** TBD
- **PRD Reference:** Section 3 (Admin Indexing Controls), Section 7 (User Story 3)
- **Key Deliverables:**
  - Admin page listing registered repositories with indexing status
  - Index trigger button per repository
  - Job status display (polling or realtime updates)
  - Admin role gate (redirect unauthenticated/unauthorized users)

1→# Sandra V1 User Stories
     2→
     3→## Summary
     4→
     5→25 user stories across 6 categories.
     6→
     7→## Traceability Matrix
     8→
     9→| US ID | Title | Feature | Task(s) | Status |
    10→|-------|-------|---------|---------|--------|
    11→| US-01 | Database schema setup | F11 | TBD | [ ] |
    12→| US-02 | Seed initial data | F11 | TBD | [ ] |
    13→| US-03 | Receive responses in preferred language | F8 | TBD | [ ] |
    14→| US-04 | Language types and validation | F8 | TBD | [ ] |
    15→| US-05 | Structured error responses | F12 | TBD | [ ] |
    16→| US-06 | Tool execution audit trail | F12 | TBD | [ ] |
    17→| US-07 | Authenticate to access Sandra | F9 | TBD | [ ] |
    18→| US-08 | Admin role verification | F9 | TBD | [ ] |
    19→| US-09 | Conversation persists during session | F7 | TBD | [ ] |
    20→| US-10 | Resume a conversation | F7 | TBD | [ ] |
    21→| US-11 | Tool registry for agent functionality | F5 | TBD | [ ] |
    22→| US-12 | Scoped tool execution | F5 | TBD | [ ] |
    23→| US-13 | Agent processes user message | F4 | TBD | [ ] |
    24→| US-14 | Agent uses tools when needed | F4 | TBD | [ ] |
    25→| US-15 | Index repository documentation | F3 | TBD | [ ] |
    26→| US-16 | Track indexing job progress | F3 | TBD | [ ] |
    27→| US-17 | Ask about EdLight initiatives | F2 | TBD | [ ] |
    28→| US-18 | Answer uses indexed knowledge | F2 | TBD | [ ] |
    29→| US-19 | Chat API endpoint | F10 | TBD | [ ] |
    30→| US-20 | Admin API endpoints | F10 | TBD | [ ] |
    31→| US-21 | Chat with Sandra in browser | F1 | TBD | [ ] |
    32→| US-22 | See streaming responses | F1 | TBD | [ ] |
    33→| US-23 | Select language in chat UI | F1 | TBD | [ ] |
    34→| US-24 | Trigger repository indexing | F6 | TBD | [ ] |
    35→| US-25 | View indexing status | F6 | TBD | [ ] |
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Stories by Category
    40→
    41→### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)
    42→
    43→#### US-01: Database Schema Setup
    44→
    45→> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.
    46→
    47→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    48→- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
    49→- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
    50→- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
    51→- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
    52→- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8
    53→
    54→**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
    55→
    56→---
    57→
    58→#### US-02: Seed Initial Data
    59→
    60→> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.
    61→
    62→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    63→- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
    64→- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
    65→- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
    66→- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`
    67→
    68→**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
    69→
    70→---
    71→
    72→#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language
    73→
    74→> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.
    75→
    76→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    77→- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
    78→- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
    79→- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
    80→- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages
    81→
    82→**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→#### US-04: Language Types and Validation
    87→
    88→> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.
    89→
    90→**Acceptance Criteria:**
    91→- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
    92→- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
    93→- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
    94→- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components
    95→
    96→**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
    97→
    98→---
    99→
   100→#### US-05: Structured Error Responses
   101→
   102→> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.
   103→
   104→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   105→- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
   106→- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
   107→- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
   108→- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals
   109→
   110→**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail
   115→
   116→> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.
   117→
   118→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   119→- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
   120→- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
   121→- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
   122→- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)
   123→
   124→**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   125→
   126→---
   127→
   128→### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)
   129→
   130→#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra
   131→
   132→> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.
   133→
   134→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   135→- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
   136→- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
   137→- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
   138→- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
   139→- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token
   140→
   141→**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   142→
   143→---
   144→
   145→#### US-08: Admin Role Verification
   146→
   147→> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.
   148→
   149→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   150→- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
   151→- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
   152→- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
   153→- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints
   154→
   155→**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   156→
   157→---
   158→
   159→#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session
   160→
   161→> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.
   162→
   163→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   164→- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
   165→- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
   166→- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
   167→- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
   168→- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation
   169→
   170→**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   171→
   172→---
   173→
   174→#### US-10: Resume a Conversation
   175→
   176→> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.
   177→
   178→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   179→- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
   180→- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
   181→- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
   182→- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created
   183→
   184→**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   185→
   186→---
   187→
   188→### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)
   189→
   190→#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality
   191→
   192→> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.
   193→
   194→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   195→- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
   196→- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
   197→- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
   198→- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
   199→- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index
   200→
   201→**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   202→
   203→---
   204→
   205→#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution
   206→
   207→> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.
   208→
   209→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   210→- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
   211→- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
   212→- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
   213→- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
   214→- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message
   215→
   216→**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   217→
   218→---
   219→
   220→#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message
   221→
   222→> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.
   223→
   224→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   225→- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
   226→- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
   227→- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
   228→- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
   229→- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
   230→- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged
   231→
   232→**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   233→
   234→---
   235→
   236→#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed
   237→
   238→> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.
   239→
   240→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   241→- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
   242→- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
   243→- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
   244→- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
   245→- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history
   246→
   247→**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   248→
   249→---
   250→
   251→### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)
   252→
   253→#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation
   254→
   255→> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.
   256→
   257→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   258→- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
   259→- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
   260→- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
   261→- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
   262→- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
   263→- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables
   264→
   265→**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   266→
   267→---
   268→
   269→#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress
   270→
   271→> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.
   272→
   273→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   274→- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
   275→- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
   276→- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
   277→- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
   278→- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID
   279→
   280→**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   281→
   282→---
   283→
   284→#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives
   285→
   286→> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.
   287→
   288→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   289→- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
   290→- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
   291→- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
   292→- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating
   293→
   294→**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   295→
   296→---
   297→
   298→#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge
   299→
   300→> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.
   301→
   302→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   303→- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
   304→- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
   305→- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
   306→- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
   307→- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent
   308→
   309→**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   310→
   311→---
   312→
   313→### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)
   314→
   315→#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint
   316→
   317→> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.
   318→
   319→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   320→- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
   321→- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
   322→- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
   323→- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
   324→- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
   325→- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing
   326→
   327→**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   328→
   329→---
   330→
   331→#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints
   332→
   333→> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.
   334→
   335→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   336→- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
   337→- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
   338→- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
   339→- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
   340→- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response
   341→
   342→**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   343→
   344→---
   345→
   346→### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)
   347→
   348→#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser
   349→
   350→> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.
   351→
   352→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   353→- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
   354→- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
   355→- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
   356→- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
   357→- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
   358→- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers
   359→
   360→**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   361→
   362→---
   363→
   364→#### US-22: See Streaming Responses
   365→
   366→> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.
   367→
   368→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   369→- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
   370→- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
   371→- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
   372→- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
   373→- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages
   374→
   375→**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   376→
   377→---
   378→
   379→#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI
   380→
   381→> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.
   382→
   383→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   384→- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
   385→- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
   386→- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
   387→- [ ] The default language is English
   388→
   389→**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   390→
   391→---
   392→
   393→#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing
   394→
   395→> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.
   396→
   397→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   398→- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
   399→- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
   400→- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
   401→- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
   402→- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
   403→- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page
   404→
   405→**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   406→
   407→---
   408→
   409→#### US-25: View Indexing Status
   410→
   411→> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.
   412→
   413→**Acceptance Criteria:**
   414→- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
   415→- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
   416→- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
   417→- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)
   418→
   419→**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have
   420→
