## Stories by Category

### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)

#### US-01: Database Schema Setup

> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T010, T011, T013 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-02: Seed Initial Data

> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T012 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language

> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T015, T016, T017, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-04: Language Types and Validation

> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T014, T015, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-05: Structured Error Responses

> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail

> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)

#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra

> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-08: Admin Role Verification

> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session

> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-10: Resume a Conversation

> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)

#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality

> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution

> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed

> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress

> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives

> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge

> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints

> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)

#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser

> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-22: See Streaming Responses

> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI

> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
- [ ] The default language is English

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing

> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-25: View Indexing Status

> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed

> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress

> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives

> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge

> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints

> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)

#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser

> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-22: See Streaming Responses

> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI

> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
- [ ] The default language is English

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing

> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-25: View Indexing Status

> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

# Sandra V1 User Stories

## Summary

25 user stories across 6 categories.

## Traceability Matrix

| US ID | Title | Feature | Task(s) | Status |
|-------|-------|---------|---------|--------|
| US-01 | Database schema setup | F11 | T010, T011, T013 | [ ] |
| US-02 | Seed initial data | F11 | T012 | [ ] |
| US-03 | Receive responses in preferred language | F8 | T015, T016, T017, T018 | [ ] |
| US-04 | Language types and validation | F8 | T014, T015, T018 | [ ] |
| US-05 | Structured error responses | F12 | T030, T031, T035 | [ ] |
| US-06 | Tool execution audit trail | F12 | T034, T035 | [ ] |
| US-07 | Authenticate to access Sandra | F9 | T036, T037, T038, T039, T041 | [ ] |
| US-08 | Admin role verification | F9 | T040, T041, T109 | [ ] |
| US-09 | Conversation persists during session | F7 | T050, T051, T052, T053, T055 | [ ] |
| US-10 | Resume a conversation | F7 | T054, T055, T127 | [ ] |
| US-11 | Tool registry for agent functionality | F5 | T056, T057, T059, T060, T061, T062, T063, T064, T065 | [ ] |
| US-12 | Scoped tool execution | F5 | T032, T033, T058, T065 | [ ] |
| US-13 | Agent processes user message | F4 | T070, T071, T073, T074, T075, T076, T077 | [ ] |
| US-14 | Agent uses tools when needed | F4 | T075, T077 | [ ] |
| US-15 | Index repository documentation | F3 | T078, T079, T080, T081, T082, T084, T085, T086, T088 | [ ] |
| US-16 | Track indexing job progress | F3 | T083, T086, T087 | [ ] |
| US-17 | Ask about EdLight initiatives | F2 | T111, T113, T114, T115 | [ ] |
| US-18 | Answer uses indexed knowledge | F2 | T111, T112, T113, T114 | [ ] |
| US-19 | Chat API endpoint | F10 | T100, T101, T102, T103, T104, T105, T110 | [ ] |
| US-20 | Admin API endpoints | F10 | T106, T107, T108, T109, T110 | [ ] |
| US-21 | Chat with Sandra in browser | F1 | T120, T121, T122, T123, T124, T126, T127, T128, T130 | [ ] |
| US-22 | See streaming responses | F1 | T104, T125, T130 | [ ] |
| US-23 | Select language in chat UI | F1 | T129, T130 | [ ] |
| US-24 | Trigger repository indexing | F6 | T131, T132, T133, T135 | [ ] |
| US-25 | View indexing status | F6 | T134, T135 | [ ] |

---

## Stories by Category

### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)

#### US-01: Database Schema Setup

> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T010, T011, T013 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-02: Seed Initial Data

> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T012 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language

> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T015, T016, T017, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-04: Language Types and Validation

> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T014, T015, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-05: Structured Error Responses

> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** T030, T031, T035 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail

> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** T034, T035 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)

#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra

> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-08: Admin Role Verification

> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session

> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-10: Resume a Conversation

> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)

#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality

> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution

> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

# Sandra V1 User Stories

## Summary

25 user stories across 6 categories.

## Traceability Matrix

## Stories by Category

### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)

#### US-01: Database Schema Setup

> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T010, T011, T013 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-02: Seed Initial Data

> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T012 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language

> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T015, T016, T017, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-04: Language Types and Validation

> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T014, T015, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-05: Structured Error Responses

> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** T030, T031, T035 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail

> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** T034, T035 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)

#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra

> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** T036, T037, T038, T039, T041 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-08: Admin Role Verification

> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session

> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-10: Resume a Conversation

> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)

#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality

> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution

> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed

> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress

> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives

> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge

> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints

> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)

#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser

> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-22: See Streaming Responses

> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI

> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
- [ ] The default language is English

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing

> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-25: View Indexing Status

> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

---

#### US-12: Scoped Tool Execution

> As a developer, I want tool execution to enforce permission scopes so that public tools and admin tools have different access levels.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Permission scopes are defined: read:public_content, admin:indexing
- [ ] Tool executor checks the caller's scopes against the tool's requiredScopes before execution
- [ ] Public tools (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo) require read:public_content
- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-13: Agent Processes User Message

> As a developer, I want the Sandra agent runtime to receive a user message, build context, call the LLM, and return a response so that all channels use a single processing path.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Agent runtime accepts an InboundMessage (content, sessionId, language, user context)
- [ ] Runtime loads session history from Firestore and builds a context window
- [ ] System prompt includes language instruction, available tools, and session context
- [ ] LLM is called via the AIProvider abstraction (OpenAI implementation)
- [ ] Final response is returned as an OutboundMessage with content and metadata
- [ ] Agent actions (LLM calls, tool executions) are logged

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-14: Agent Uses Tools When Needed

> As a developer, I want the agent to detect when a tool call is needed, execute it, and incorporate the result so that Sandra can answer questions requiring data retrieval.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] When the LLM response includes a tool call, the runtime executes the tool via the registry
- [ ] Tool results are fed back into the LLM for the next iteration of the agent loop
- [ ] The agent loop continues until the LLM produces a final text response (no more tool calls)
- [ ] A maximum iteration limit prevents infinite loops (e.g., 5 iterations)
- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

**Feature:** F4 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Knowledge Pipeline (US-15 through US-18)

#### US-15: Index Repository Documentation

> As an administrator, I want to index README and documentation files from EdLight repositories so that Sandra can answer questions using up-to-date content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GitHub API client fetches README.md and files in docs/ directories for a given repository
- [ ] Fetched markdown content is extracted as plain text
- [ ] Documents are split into chunks using recursive character splitting with markdown-aware boundaries
- [ ] Each chunk is embedded using the embedding service (OpenAI text-embedding-3-small)
- [ ] Embeddings are stored in the vector store with source metadata (repo, file path, chunk index)
- [ ] Metadata is recorded in indexed_sources and indexed_documents Postgres tables

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-16: Track Indexing Job Progress

> As an administrator, I want indexing jobs tracked with status updates so that I know when indexing completes or fails.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Triggering an index creates an index_jobs record with status "pending"
- [ ] Status transitions through pending → running → completed or failed
- [ ] Completed jobs record the number of documents and chunks indexed
- [ ] Failed jobs record the error message
- [ ] Job status is queryable by job ID

**Feature:** F3 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-17: Ask About EdLight Initiatives

> As a visitor, I want to ask Sandra questions about EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, and EdLight Initiative so that I can learn about available programs.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Asking "What is EdLight Code?" returns a response that references indexed EdLight Code documentation
- [ ] Asking "What programs does EdLight offer?" returns information about multiple initiatives
- [ ] Responses are conversational and accurate based on indexed content
- [ ] If no relevant knowledge is found, Sandra indicates it does not have that information rather than hallucinating

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-18: Answer Uses Indexed Knowledge

> As a visitor, I want Sandra's answers to be grounded in indexed repository content so that the information is accurate and verifiable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] The retrieval service converts the user query into an embedding and performs similarity search
- [ ] Top-k relevant chunks are injected into the agent's context window
- [ ] The LLM prompt instructs the model to answer based on provided context
- [ ] Retrieved chunks include source metadata (repository name, file path)
- [ ] The searchKnowledgeBase tool is wired to the retrieval service and invoked by the agent

**Feature:** F2 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### API Layer (US-19 through US-20)

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

> As a developer, I want a POST /api/sandra/chat endpoint so that the web UI and future channels can send messages to Sandra through a standard API.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/chat accepts { sessionId, message, language } with Zod validation
- [ ] Endpoint returns { reply, sessionId, messageId } in the standard JSON envelope
- [ ] Streaming responses are supported via SSE (Server-Sent Events) when Accept header requests it
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/conversations/:sessionId returns the message history for a session
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/health returns { status: "ok" } with service version
- [ ] All responses include a request ID for tracing

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-20: Admin API Endpoints

> As an administrator, I want API endpoints for repository management so that admin UI and scripts can manage indexing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/repos returns the list of registered repositories with their indexing status
- [ ] POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index triggers an indexing job and returns the job ID
- [ ] GET /api/sandra/index-jobs/:jobId returns the current status of an indexing job
- [ ] All admin endpoints require a valid Firebase Auth token with admin role
- [ ] Unauthorized requests receive a 401 or 403 response

**Feature:** F10 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### User Interface (US-21 through US-25)

#### US-21: Chat With Sandra in Browser

> As a visitor, I want to open Sandra in my browser and type messages so that I can ask questions about EdLight.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A chat page is accessible at the Sandra web URL
- [ ] The page displays a message input field and a send button
- [ ] Submitted messages appear in the chat history immediately
- [ ] Sandra's responses appear in the chat history after processing
- [ ] An empty/welcome state displays branding and suggested starter questions
- [ ] The layout is responsive and works on mobile and desktop browsers

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-22: See Streaming Responses

> As a visitor, I want to see Sandra's response appear word-by-word as it generates so that I know the system is working and I can start reading immediately.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Responses stream to the UI token-by-token as they are generated by the LLM
- [ ] A typing/loading indicator is shown while waiting for the first token
- [ ] If streaming fails mid-response, an error message is displayed
- [ ] The chat input is disabled while a response is being generated
- [ ] Completed responses are stored in message history and render identically to non-streamed messages

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-23: Select Language in Chat UI

> As a visitor, I want to select my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) in the chat interface so that Sandra responds in my language.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A language selector is visible in the chat UI (dropdown or toggle)
- [ ] Selecting a language sends it as the language parameter on subsequent chat requests
- [ ] The selected language persists for the duration of the session
- [ ] The default language is English

**Feature:** F1 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-24: Trigger Repository Indexing

> As an administrator, I want to click a button to trigger indexing for a repository so that Sandra's knowledge base stays current.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin page displays a list of registered repositories
- [ ] Each repository row has an "Index Now" button
- [ ] Clicking the button calls POST /api/sandra/repos/:repoId/index
- [ ] A success message confirms the indexing job was started
- [ ] The button is disabled while an indexing job is already running for that repository
- [ ] Only authenticated admin users can access the admin page

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-25: View Indexing Status

> As an administrator, I want to see the current indexing status for each repository so that I know which repositories are up to date.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each repository in the admin list shows its last indexed timestamp
- [ ] Active indexing jobs show a progress indicator (pending, running, completed, failed)
- [ ] Failed jobs display the error reason
- [ ] The status updates without requiring a full page refresh (polling or realtime)

**Feature:** F6 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

# Sandra V1 User Stories

## Summary

25 user stories across 6 categories.

## Traceability Matrix

| US ID | Title | Feature | Task(s) | Status |
|-------|-------|---------|---------|--------|
| US-01 | Database schema setup | F11 | T010, T011, T013 | [ ] |
| US-02 | Seed initial data | F11 | T012 | [ ] |
| US-03 | Receive responses in preferred language | F8 | T015, T016, T017, T018 | [ ] |
| US-04 | Language types and validation | F8 | T014, T015, T018 | [ ] |
| US-05 | Structured error responses | F12 | T030, T031, T035 | [ ] |
| US-06 | Tool execution audit trail | F12 | T034, T035 | [ ] |
| US-07 | Authenticate to access Sandra | F9 | T036, T037, T038, T039, T041 | [ ] |
| US-08 | Admin role verification | F9 | T040, T041, T109 | [ ] |
| US-09 | Conversation persists during session | F7 | T050, T051, T052, T053, T055 | [ ] |
| US-10 | Resume a conversation | F7 | T054, T055, T127 | [ ] |
| US-11 | Tool registry for agent functionality | F5 | T056, T057, T059, T060, T061, T062, T063, T064, T065 | [ ] |
| US-12 | Scoped tool execution | F5 | T032, T033, T058, T065 | [ ] |
| US-13 | Agent processes user message | F4 | T070, T071, T073, T074, T075, T076, T077 | [ ] |
| US-14 | Agent uses tools when needed | F4 | T075, T077 | [ ] |
| US-15 | Index repository documentation | F3 | T078, T079, T080, T081, T082, T084, T085, T086, T088 | [ ] |
| US-16 | Track indexing job progress | F3 | T083, T086, T087 | [ ] |
| US-17 | Ask about EdLight initiatives | F2 | T111, T113, T114, T115 | [ ] |
| US-18 | Answer uses indexed knowledge | F2 | T111, T112, T113, T114 | [ ] |
| US-19 | Chat API endpoint | F10 | T100, T101, T102, T103, T104, T105, T110 | [ ] |
| US-20 | Admin API endpoints | F10 | T106, T107, T108, T109, T110 | [ ] |
| US-21 | Chat with Sandra in browser | F1 | T120, T121, T122, T123, T124, T126, T127, T128, T130 | [ ] |
| US-22 | See streaming responses | F1 | T104, T125, T130 | [ ] |
| US-23 | Select language in chat UI | F1 | T129, T130 | [ ] |
| US-24 | Trigger repository indexing | F6 | T131, T132, T133, T135 | [ ] |
| US-25 | View indexing status | F6 | T134, T135 | [ ] |

---

## Stories by Category

### Foundation & Infrastructure (US-01 through US-06)

#### US-01: Database Schema Setup

> As a developer, I want the Postgres database schema defined and migrated so that all services have reliable structured storage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Prisma schema defines all MVP tables: users, initiatives, repo_registry, indexed_sources, indexed_documents, index_jobs, tool_executions, audit_logs
- [ ] Running `npx prisma migrate dev` succeeds and creates all tables
- [ ] Prisma client is generated with typed models for each table
- [ ] Environment config validates DATABASE_URL with Zod at startup
- [ ] Schema matches the data model in PRD section 8

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T010, T011, T013 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-02: Seed Initial Data

> As a developer, I want the four EdLight repositories and initiative records pre-seeded so that the system has baseline data without manual entry.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Seed script creates repo_registry entries for edlinitiative/code, EdLight-News, EdLight-Initiative, EdLight-Academy
- [ ] Seed script creates initiative records for EdLight Code, EdLight News, EdLight Academy, EdLight Initiative
- [ ] Running the seed script is idempotent (re-running does not create duplicates)
- [ ] Seed script can be invoked via `npx prisma db seed`

**Feature:** F11 | **Tasks:** T012 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-03: Receive Responses in Preferred Language

> As a visitor, I want to receive Sandra's responses in my preferred language (English, French, or Haitian Creole) so that I can understand the information.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Language parameter (en, fr, ht) is accepted on each chat request
- [ ] Sandra's system prompt instructs the LLM to respond in the requested language
- [ ] If no language is specified, Sandra defaults to English
- [ ] Language-specific prompt templates exist for all three supported languages

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T015, T016, T017, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-04: Language Types and Validation

> As a developer, I want language enums, types, and Zod schemas defined so that language handling is consistent across the codebase.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SupportedLanguage enum defines en, fr, ht values
- [ ] Zod schema validates language input and rejects unsupported values
- [ ] Language utility functions are exported from a single module (src/lib/config or src/lib/utils)
- [ ] Type definitions are reusable by API routes, agent runtime, and UI components

**Feature:** F8 | **Tasks:** T014, T015, T018 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-05: Structured Error Responses

> As a developer, I want all errors to flow through SandraError subclasses so that API consumers receive consistent, actionable error messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] SandraError base class exists with code, message, and statusCode properties
- [ ] Subclasses exist: ValidationError (400), AuthError (401), ForbiddenError (403), NotFoundError (404), ToolError (500)
- [ ] API routes catch SandraError instances and return structured JSON with error code and message
- [ ] Unhandled errors are caught and returned as generic 500 responses without leaking internals

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** T030, T031, T035 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-06: Tool Execution Audit Trail

> As an administrator, I want all tool executions and admin actions logged so that I can audit system behavior.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Every tool execution writes a record to the tool_executions table with tool name, input, output, user context, and timestamp
- [ ] Admin actions (trigger indexing, etc.) write records to the audit_logs table
- [ ] Audit records include the request ID for traceability
- [ ] Logging does not block the main request flow (failures are caught and logged, not re-thrown)

**Feature:** F12 | **Tasks:** T034, T035 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Authentication & Sessions (US-07 through US-10)

#### US-07: Authenticate to Access Sandra

> As a visitor, I want to optionally sign in with Firebase Auth so that my identity is recognized for session continuity.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Firebase Admin SDK is initialized on the server with validated environment config
- [ ] API routes accept a Bearer token in the Authorization header
- [ ] Valid Firebase tokens are decoded and the user's Firebase UID is extracted
- [ ] If a Postgres user record does not exist for the Firebase UID, one is created (user resolution flow)
- [ ] Unauthenticated requests to public endpoints (chat, health) are allowed without a token

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** T036, T037, T038, T039, T041 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-08: Admin Role Verification

> As an administrator, I want admin-only endpoints to verify my admin role so that unauthorized users cannot trigger indexing or access admin features.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Admin role is stored on the Postgres user record
- [ ] Auth middleware checks the admin role before allowing access to admin endpoints
- [ ] Non-admin authenticated users receive a 403 Forbidden response on admin endpoints
- [ ] Unauthenticated users receive a 401 Unauthorized response on admin endpoints

**Feature:** F9 | **Tasks:** T040, T041, T109 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-09: Conversation Persists During Session

> As a visitor, I want my conversation with Sandra to persist during the session so that I can scroll back and see previous messages.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] A new session ID is generated when a conversation starts
- [ ] Each message (user and assistant) is stored in Firestore at chat_sessions/{sessionId}/messages
- [ ] Messages are stored with role, content, timestamp, and messageId
- [ ] Retrieving a session returns messages in chronological order
- [ ] Session data is accessible for the duration of the conversation

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** T050, T051, T052, T053, T055 | **Priority:** Must-have

---

#### US-10: Resume a Conversation

> As a visitor, I want to resume a previous conversation by providing a session ID so that I can continue asking follow-up questions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Providing an existing session ID in a chat request loads prior message history
- [ ] The agent receives recent message history as context for generating responses
- [ ] Follow-up questions correctly reference information from earlier in the conversation
- [ ] Invalid or non-existent session IDs result in a new session being created

**Feature:** F7 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

---

### Agent Core (US-11 through US-14)

#### US-11: Tool Registry for Agent Functionality

> As a developer, I want a self-registering tool registry so that the agent can discover and invoke tools at runtime without hardcoded references.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Tool interface defines name, description, inputSchema (Zod), requiredScopes, and handler
- [ ] Tool registry provides register() and getAll() methods
- [ ] Each MVP tool (searchKnowledgeBase, getEdLightInitiatives, lookupRepoInfo, triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) self-registers on import
- [ ] Registry exports tool definitions in a format consumable by the LLM (name, description, parameters)
- [ ] Adding a new tool requires only: create file, implement interface, import in index

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

- [ ] Admin tools (triggerRepoIndexing, getIndexingStatus) require admin:indexing
- [ ] Scope violations return a ForbiddenError with a descriptive message

**Feature:** F5 | **Tasks:** TBD | **Priority:** Must-have

- [ ] Tool call results are included in the session message history

#### US-19: Chat API Endpoint

25 user stories across 6 categories.

## Traceability Matrix

| US ID | Title | Feature | Task(s) | Status |
|-------|-------|---------|---------|--------|
